#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>
#include <string_view>

struct PacketHeader {
    uint8_t version;
    uint8_t flags;
    uint16_t packet_id;
};

PacketHeader read_packet_header(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    return *reinterpret_cast<const PacketHeader*>(buffer.data());
}

std::string_view read_message_until_delimiter(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    const char* data = reinterpret_cast<const char*>(buffer.data());
    size_t length = 0;
    for (size_t i = 0; i < buffer.size(); ++i) {
        if (data[i] == '\0') {
            length = i;
            break;
        }
    }
    return std::string_view(data, length);
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

std::vector<uint8_t> read_buffer_data(const std::vector<uint8_t>& source_buffer, size_t bytes_to_read) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> result(bytes_to_read);
    hexi::buffer_adaptor adaptor(source_buffer.data(), source_buffer.size());
    adaptor.read(result.data(), bytes_to_read);
    return result;
}

void write_buffer_data(std::vector<uint8_t>& dest_buffer, const std::vector<uint8_t>& source_data) {
    dest_buffer.resize(source_data.size());
    hexi::buffer_adaptor adaptor(dest_buffer.data(), dest_buffer.size());
    adaptor.write(source_data.data(), source_data.size());
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <cstddef>

const uint8_t* get_read_pointer(const std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer) {
    return buffer.data();
}

uint8_t* get_write_pointer(std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer) {
    return buffer.data() + buffer.size();
}

struct BufferInfo {
    size_t readable_size;
    size_t free_space;
};

BufferInfo check_buffer_space(const std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer) {
    return {buffer.size(), buffer.capacity() - buffer.size()};
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

bool write_data_with_check(std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer, uint32_t header, const std::vector<uint32_t>& items) {
    // Calculate the total space needed
    size_t required_space = sizeof(header) + items.size() * sizeof(uint32_t);
    
    // Check if the buffer has sufficient capacity
    if (buffer.capacity() - buffer.size() < required_space) {
        return false;
    }
    
    // Create a buffer adaptor to manage the buffer operations
    hexi::buffer_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    
    // Verify available space through the adaptor
    if (adaptor.available() < required_space) {
        return false;
    }
    
    // Get the current write pointer
    uint8_t* write_ptr = adaptor.write_ptr();
    
    // Initialize a binary stream for writing data
    hexi::binary_stream stream(write_ptr, required_space);
    
    // Write the header to the stream
    stream.write(&header, sizeof(header));
    
    // Write the items container to the stream
    stream.write_container(items);
    
    // Update the buffer size to include the newly written data
    buffer.resize(buffer.size() + required_space);
    
    return true;
}

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>
#include <stdexcept>

struct PacketHeader {
    uint32_t magic;
    uint16_t version;
    uint16_t flags;
};

PacketHeader read_header_with_span(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::buffer_adaptor adaptor(buffer.data(), buffer.size());

    if (adaptor.available() < sizeof(PacketHeader)) {
        throw std::runtime_error("Insufficient data for packet header");
    }

    PacketHeader header;

    stream.read(&header.magic, sizeof(header.magic));
    stream.read(&header.version, sizeof(header.version));
    stream.read(&header.flags, sizeof(header.flags));
    
    return header;
}

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <string>

struct UserData {
    uint32_t user_id;
    std::string name;
    
    void serialise(auto& adaptor) {
        adaptor(user_id, name);
    }
};

std::vector<uint8_t> write_and_serialise_packet(uint32_t header, const UserData& data) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    hexi::BufferAdaptor adaptor(buffer);
    adaptor.write(header);
    data.serialise(adaptor);
    return buffer;
}
```</answer>

MessagePacket read_header_and_message(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::buffer_adaptor adaptor(buffer);

    if (null_pos == std::string_view::npos) {
        // If no null terminator found, use the entire view

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/dynamic_buffer.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <cstring>

template<size_t block_size = 1024>
void write_to_dynamic_buffer(hexi::dynamic_buffer<block_size>& buffer, uint32_t header, const std::vector<uint8_t>& data) {
    buffer.write(reinterpret_cast<const uint8_t*>(&header), sizeof(header));
    buffer.write(data.data(), data.size());
}

template<size_t block_size = 1024>
struct BufferData {
    uint32_t header;
    std::vector<uint8_t> data;
};

BufferData<> read_from_dynamic_buffer(hexi::dynamic_buffer<1024>& buffer, size_t data_length) {
    buffer.seek(0);
    
    uint32_t header;
    uint8_t header_bytes[4];
    buffer.read(header_bytes, 4);
    std::memcpy(&header, header_bytes, sizeof(header));
    
    std::vector<uint8_t> data(data_length);
    buffer.read(data.data(), data_length);
    
    return BufferData<> { header, data };
}
```</answer>

<think>Okay, I need to implement the convert_with_enum function using the Hexi library. Let me think about how the conversion enum works here.

First, the function takes a template parameter conv which is of type hexi::endian::conversion. The possible values are big_to_native, native_to_big, little_to_native, native_to_little. So depending on which conversion is specified, I have to apply the corresponding endian conversion.

Looking at the Hexi library's documentation (assuming it's similar to other endian libraries), there should be functions like hexi::endian::native_to_big, native_to_little, big_to_native, and little_to_native. These functions probably take a value and return it converted to the target endianness.

So for each case in the enum, I can map it to the appropriate Hexi function. Let's see:

- If conv is hexi::endian::conversion::big_to_native, then use hexi::endian::big_to_native(value)
- Similarly for native_to_big: hexi::endian::native_to_big(value)
- For little_to_native: hexi::endian::little_to_native(value)
- And native_to_little: hexi::endian::native_to_little(value)

Wait, but the enum values are named exactly as the functions? Let me check. The problem states that the enum has values like big_to_native, etc., so the template parameter conv will be one of these enum values. So the function needs to select the correct conversion function based on the enum value.

But since this is a template function, the compiler will instantiate the correct code based on the template parameter. So inside the function, we can just call the corresponding Hexi function.

So the implementation would be something like:

if conv is big_to_native, return hexi::endian::big_to_native(value)
if conv is native_to_big, return hexi::endian::native_to_big(value)
and so on for the other cases.

But how do we handle the template parameter? Since the function is templated on conv, we can use if constexpr or switch cases. Wait, but in C++, switch cases can't be used with enum template parameters directly unless we have a way to get the enum value. Alternatively, we can use if constexpr with the enum values.

Wait, the enum is part of the template parameter, so perhaps we can write:

template<hexi::endian::conversion conv, typename T>
T convert_with_enum(T value) {
    if constexpr(conv == hexi::endian::conversion::big_to_native) {
        return hexi::endian::big_to_native(value);
    } else if constexpr(conv == hexi::endian::conversion::native_to_big) {
        return hexi::endian::native_to_big(value);
    } else if constexpr(conv == hexi::endian::conversion::little_to_native) {
        return hexi::endian::little_to_native(value);
    } else if constexpr(conv == hexi::endian::conversion::native_to_little) {
        return hexi::endian::native_to_little(value);
    }
}

Yes, that makes sense. Each case checks the template parameter and calls the corresponding Hexi function. This way, the code is efficient because the compiler will inline the correct function based on the template parameter.

Also, the function is templated on T, so it should work with any type that the Hexi functions support, probably integral types like uint16_t, uint32_t, etc.

So putting it all together, the code inside the function uses if constexpr to select the right conversion function from the Hexi library based on the template parameter conv.
output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/endian.h>
#include <cstdint>

template<hexi::endian::conversion conv, typename T>
T convert_with_enum(T value) {
    if constexpr(conv == hexi::endian::conversion::big_to_native) {
        return hexi::endian::big_to_native(value);
    } else if constexpr(conv == hexi::endian::conversion::native_to_big) {
        return hexi::endian::native_to_big(value);
    } else if constexpr(conv == hexi::endian::conversion::little_to_native) {
        return hexi::endian::little_to_native(value);
    } else if constexpr(conv == hexi::endian::conversion::native_to_little) {
        return hexi::endian::native_to_little(value);
    }
}
```</answer>

    size_t readable = storage.readable_bytes();

    if (bytes_to_copy > 0) {

    }

template<size_t block_size = 1024>
struct BufferStatus {
    size_t readable_size;
    size_t free_space;
};

BufferStatus<> check_buffer_status(const hexi::detail::intrusive_storage<1024>& storage) {
    BufferStatus status;
    status.readable_size = storage.readable_bytes();
    status.free_space = 1024 - status.readable_size;
    return status;
}

template<size_t block_size = 1024>
void reset_buffer(hexi::detail::intrusive_storage<block_size>& storage) {
    storage.reset();
}

std::vector<uint8_t> read_file_data(const std::filesystem::path& file_path, size_t bytes_to_read) {

    size_t bytes_to_read_actual = std::min(bytes_to_read, file_size);
    std::vector<uint8_t> result(bytes_to_read_actual);
    size_t bytes_read = buffer.read(result.data(), bytes_to_read_actual);
    result.resize(bytes_read);
    return result;
}

std::vector<uint8_t> preview_file_data(const std::filesystem::path& file_path, size_t bytes_to_preview) {
    auto buffer = hexi::file_buffer::open_read(file_path);
    size_t file_size = buffer.size();
    size_t bytes_to_preview_actual = std::min(bytes_to_preview, file_size);
    std::vector<uint8_t> result(bytes_to_preview_actual);
    buffer.copy(result.data(), bytes_to_preview_actual); // Assuming 'copy' method exists
    return result;
}

void write_file_data(const std::filesystem::path& file_path, const std::vector<uint8_t>& data) {
    auto buffer = hexi::file_buffer::open_write(file_path);
    buffer.write(data.data(), data.size());
}

size_t get_file_size(const std::filesystem::path& file_path) {
    auto buffer = hexi::file_buffer::open_read(file_path);
    return buffer.size();
}

#include <hexi/file_buffer.h>

#include <cstdint>
#include <filesystem>

std::vector<uint8_t> preview_file_header(const std::filesystem::path& file_path, size_t header_size) {
    hexi::file_buffer file(file_path);
    std::vector<uint8_t> header_data(header_size, 0); // Initialize with zeros

    // Read the header data into the temporary buffer
    file.read(temp_buffer.data(), header_size);
    
    // Reset the file read pointer to the beginning
    file.seek(0);
    
    // Copy the temporary buffer to the result
    header_data.assign(temp_buffer.begin(), temp_buffer.end());
    
    return header_data;
}

#include <hexi/static_buffer.h>

template<size_t buffer_size = 1024>
struct BufferInfo {
    size_t capacity;
    size_t current_size;
    size_t free_space;
};

#include <hexi/pmc/stream_base.h>
#include <hexi/shared.h>

size_t get_stream_size(const hexi::pmc::stream_base& stream) {

}

bool is_stream_empty(const hexi::pmc::stream_base& stream) {
    return get_stream_size(stream) == 0;
}

struct StreamInfo {
    hexi::stream_state state;
    size_t buffer_size;
    bool is_empty;
};

StreamInfo get_stream_state_info(const hexi::pmc::stream_base& stream) {
    StreamInfo info;
    info.state = stream.get_state();
    info.buffer_size = get_stream_size(stream);
    info.is_empty = is_stream_empty(stream);
    return info;
}

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_writer.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_reader.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

struct UserData {
    uint32_t user_id;
    std::string name;
    
    void serialise(auto& stream) {
        stream << user_id << name;
    }
};

void serialize_object(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_writer& writer, const UserData& data) {
    data.serialise(writer);
}

void deserialize_object(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_reader& reader, UserData& data) {
    data.serialise(reader);
}
```

**Explanation:**

1. **Serialization:** The `serialize_object` function uses the `binary_stream_writer` to serialize the `UserData` object by calling its `serialise` method. The `serialise` method uses the stream's `<<` operator to write the `user_id` (as a `uint32_t`) and `name` (as a `std::string`) to the binary stream.

2. **Deserialization:** The `deserialize_object` function uses the `binary_stream_reader` to deserialize the `UserData` object. The same `serialise` method is called, but now with a reader. The `auto&` parameter resolves to `binary_stream_reader`, and the `<<` operator is replaced by the reader's `>>` operator (implicitly) to read the data back into the object.

**Note:** The solution assumes that the `serialise` method is designed to work bidirectionally by leveraging the appropriate stream operators (`<<` for writing, `>>` for reading) based on the stream type passed to it. This is made possible by C++'s template type deduction with `auto&`.</answer>

#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_writer.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_reader.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

void write_string_to_stream(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_writer& writer, const std::string& str) {
    writer.write_uint32(static_cast<uint32_t>(str.length()));
    writer.write_bytes(str.data(), str.length());
}

std::string read_string_from_stream(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_reader& reader) {
    uint32_t length = reader.read_uint32();
    std::vector<char> buffer(length);
    reader.read_bytes(buffer.data(), length);
    return std::string(buffer.begin(), buffer.end());
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_writer.h>

void write_container_to_stream(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_writer& writer, const std::vector<uint32_t>& container) {

#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>

#include <cstdint>

struct BufferInfo {

};

}

void read_data_from_buffer(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, uint32_t& value, void* data, std::size_t length) {

}

        if (buffer[i] == value) {
            return true;
        }
    }
    return false;
}

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

void skip_bytes_in_buffer(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, std::size_t bytes) {
    buffer.skip(bytes);
}

struct MessageHeader {
    uint16_t message_type;
    uint32_t body_length;
};

MessageHeader read_header_and_skip_body(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer) {
    MessageHeader header;
    header.message_type = buffer.read<uint16_t>();
    header.body_length = buffer.read<uint32_t>();
    buffer.skip(header.body_length);
    return header;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

const void* get_read_pointer(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer) {
    return buffer.read_ptr();
}

struct BufferState {
    std::size_t size;
    const void* read_ptr;
    bool is_empty;
};

BufferState get_buffer_state(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer) {
    BufferState state;
    state.size = buffer.size();
    state.read_ptr = buffer.read_ptr();
    state.is_empty = buffer.empty();
    return state;
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <hexi/shared.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <string>

bool check_can_write_seek(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer) {
    return buffer.can_write_seek();
}

void seek_write_position(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, hexi::buffer_seek direction, std::size_t offset) {
    buffer.write_seek(direction, offset);
}

void update_header_at_position(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, uint32_t header_value) {
    // Save current position
    auto current_pos = buffer.tell();
    
    // Write placeholder header (e.g., 0xFFFFFFFF)
    uint32_t placeholder = 0xFFFFFFFF;
    buffer.write(reinterpret_cast<const std::byte*>(&placeholder), sizeof(placeholder));
    
    // Write body data (example: "Hello World")
    std::string body = "Hello World";
    buffer.write(reinterpret_cast<const std::byte*>(body.data()), body.size());
    
    // Seek back to the saved position
    buffer.write_seek(hexi::buffer_seek::sk_absolute, current_pos);
    
    // Write the actual header value
    buffer.write(reinterpret_cast<const std::byte*>(&header_value), sizeof(header_value));
}
```</answer>

```cpp
#include <hexi/shared.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_writer.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_reader.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

using namespace hexi::detail;

template<typename T>
std::size_t encode_varint(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_writer& writer, T value) {
    using UnsignedT = std::make_unsigned_t<T>;
    UnsignedT unsigned_value = static_cast<UnsignedT>(value);
    std::size_t bytes_written = 0;
    
    while (unsigned_value > 0x7F) {
        uint8_t byte = static_cast<uint8_t>(unsigned_value & 0x7F);
        byte |= 0x80; // set MSB
        writer.write_byte(byte);
        unsigned_value >>= 7;
        bytes_written++;
    }
    
    // Write the final byte
    uint8_t final_byte = static_cast<uint8_t>(unsigned_value);
    writer.write_byte(final_byte);
    return ++bytes_written;
}

template<typename T>
T decode_varint(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_reader& reader) {
    using UnsignedT = std::make_unsigned_t<T>;
    UnsignedT result = 0;
    int shift = 0;
    
    while (true) {
        uint8_t byte = reader.read_byte();
        result |= static_cast<UnsignedT>(byte & 0x7F) << shift;
        shift += 7;
        
        if ((byte & 0x80) == 0) {
            break;
        }
    }
    
    return static_cast<T>(result);
}

template<typename T>
bool encode_decode_roundtrip(T original_value) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor write_adaptor(buffer);
    hexi::pmc::binary_stream_writer writer(write_adaptor);
    encode_varint(writer, original_value);
    
    hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor read_adaptor(buffer);
    hexi::pmc::binary_stream_reader reader(read_adaptor);
    T decoded_value = decode_varint<T>(reader);
    
    return original_value == decoded_value;
}
```

- Using `std::make_unsigned_t<T>` to handle signed integers by treating them as unsigned during encoding/decoding.

- Properly setting and checking the MSB for each byte.

- Creating separate buffer adaptors for writing and reading to ensure the reader starts from the beginning.

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/shared.h>
#include <cstring>
#include <cstddef>

using namespace hexi::detail;

bool check_regions_overlap(const void* src, std::size_t src_len, const void* dst, std::size_t dst_len) {
    const char* src_start = static_cast<const char*>(src);
    const char* src_end = src_start + src_len;
    const char* dst_start = static_cast<const char*>(dst);
    const char* dst_end = dst_start + dst_len;
    
    return (src_start < dst_end) && (dst_start < src_end);
}

bool safe_memory_copy(void* destination, const void* source, std::size_t length) {
    if (check_regions_overlap(source, length, destination, length)) {
        // Overlap detected, use memmove to handle it safely
        std::memmove(destination, source, length);
        return true;
    } else {
        // No overlap, use memcpy for better performance
        std::memcpy(destination, source, length);
        return true;
    }
}
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/shared.h>
#include <array>
#include <cstdint>

using namespace hexi::detail;

template<std::size_t Size>
constexpr auto create_filled_array(std::uint8_t value) {
    return generate_filled<Size>(value);
}

bool verify_filled_array(const auto& arr, std::uint8_t expected_value) {
    for (const auto& element : arr) {
        if (element != expected_value) {
            return false;
        }
    }
    return true;
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/exception.h>
#include <string>
#include <cstddef>

struct ExceptionDetails {
    std::size_t read_size;
    std::size_t total_read;
    std::size_t limit_or_size;
    std::string message;
};

ExceptionDetails extract_underrun_info(const hexi::buffer_underrun& e) {
    ExceptionDetails details;
    details.read_size = e.read_size();
    details.total_read = e.total_read();
    details.limit_or_size = e.buff_size();
    details.message = e.what();
    return details;
}

ExceptionDetails extract_read_limit_info(const hexi::stream_read_limit& e) {
    ExceptionDetails details;
    details.read_size = e.read_size();
    details.total_read = e.total_read();
    details.limit_or_size = e.read_limit();
    details.message = e.what();
    return details;
}

std::string get_exception_message(const hexi::exception& e) {
    return e.what();
}

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <string>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

struct CompactPacket {
    uint32_t packet_id;
    std::vector<std::string> tags;
    std::string data;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_compact_packet(const CompactPacket& packet) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    
    // Serialize packet_id as varint
    hexi::varint<std::uint32_t>().serialize(packet.packet_id, buffer);
    
    // Serialize tags using prefixed_varint
    hexi::prefixed_varint<std::vector<std::string>>().serialize(packet.tags, buffer);
    
    // Serialize data using prefixed_varint
    hexi::prefixed_varint<std::string>().serialize(packet.data, buffer);
    
    return buffer;
}

CompactPacket deserialize_compact_packet(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    CompactPacket packet;
    size_t offset = 0;
    
    // Deserialize packet_id
    packet.packet_id = hexi::varint<std::uint32_t>().deserialize(buffer.data(), buffer.size(), offset);
    
    // Deserialize tags vector
    packet.tags = hexi::prefixed_varint<std::vector<std::string>>().deserialize(buffer.data(), buffer.size(), offset);
    
    // Deserialize data string
    packet.data = hexi::prefixed_varint<std::string>().deserialize(buffer.data(), buffer.size(), offset);
    
    return packet;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <string>
#include <cstdint>
#include <vector>

struct PaddedData {
    uint16_t header;
    std::string data;
    uint32_t footer;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_with_padding(const PaddedData& packet) {
    hexi::allocators::default_allocator alloc;
    hexi::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<uint8_t>> adaptor;
    hexi::stream stream(alloc, adaptor);
    
    // Write header
    stream << packet.header;
    
    // Write data length as uint32_t
    uint32_t data_len = static_cast<uint32_t>(packet.data.length());
    stream << data_len;
    
    // Write data bytes
    stream.write(packet.data.data(), data_len);
    
    // Write 16 bytes of padding filled with 0x00
    stream.fill<16>(0x00);
    
    // Write footer
    stream << packet.footer;
    
    return adaptor->get_buffer();
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/allocators/block_allocator.h>
#include <vector>
#include <memory>

struct PoolObject {
    int id;
    double value;
    
    // Default constructor required by block_allocator
    PoolObject() : id(0), value(0.0) {}
    
    PoolObject(int i, double v) : id(i), value(v) {}
};

std::vector<PoolObject*> create_object_pool(hexi::block_allocator<PoolObject, 10>& allocator) {
    std::vector<PoolObject*> objects;
    
    // Allocate and construct the first object
    PoolObject* obj1 = allocator.allocate();
    new (obj1) PoolObject(1, 10.0);
    objects.push_back(obj1);
    
    // Allocate and construct the second object
    PoolObject* obj2 = allocator.allocate();
    new (obj2) PoolObject(2, 20.0);
    objects.push_back(obj2);
    
    // Allocate and construct the third object
    PoolObject* obj3 = allocator.allocate();
    new (obj3) PoolObject(3, 30.0);
    objects.push_back(obj3);
    
    return objects;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_base.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstddef>

class CustomBuffer : public hexi::pmc::buffer_base {
    std::vector<std::byte> data_;
    
public:
    std::size_t size() const override {
        return data_.size();
    }
    
    bool empty() const override {
        return data_.empty();
    }
    
    // Helper method to add data (for testing)
    void add_data(const void* source, std::size_t length) {
        const std::byte* bytes = static_cast<const std::byte*>(source);
        data_.insert(data_.end(), bytes, bytes + length);
    }
    
    // Helper method to clear data (for testing)
    void clear() {
        data_.clear();
    }
};
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <hexi/endian.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

struct ProtocolPacket {
    uint32_t message_id;
    std::vector<uint16_t> data_values;
    uint32_t checksum;
};

struct ProtocolHeader {
    uint32_t message_id;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_protocol_packet(const ProtocolPacket& packet) {
    hexi::allocators::default_allocator alloc;
    hexi::buffers::memory_buffer buffer;
    hexi::streams::binary_stream stream(buffer, alloc);

    // Convert and write message_id
    uint32_t be_message_id = hexi::endian::native_to_big(packet.message_id);
    stream.write(be_message_id);

    // Convert and write data_values
    std::vector<uint16_t> be_data_values;
    for (const auto& val : packet.data_values) {
        be_data_values.push_back(hexi::endian::native_to_big(val));
    }
    stream.write_container(be_data_values);

    // Convert and write checksum
    uint32_t be_checksum = hexi::endian::native_to_big(packet.checksum);
    stream.write(be_checksum);

    return buffer.data();
}

ProtocolPacket deserialize_protocol_packet(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::allocators::default_allocator alloc;
    hexi::buffers::raw_buffer read_buf(buffer.data(), buffer.size());
    hexi::streams::binary_stream_reader reader(read_buf, alloc);
    ProtocolPacket packet;

    // Read and convert message_id
    uint32_t be_message_id;
    reader.read(be_message_id);
    packet.message_id = hexi::endian::big_to_native(be_message_id);

    // Read and convert data_values count
    uint32_t be_count;
    reader.read(be_count);
    uint32_t count = hexi::endian::big_to_native(be_count);

    // Read data_values container
    std::vector<uint16_t> data_values;
    reader.read_container(data_values);

    // Convert each element in data_values
    for (auto& val : data_values) {
        val = hexi::endian::big_to_native(val);
    }
    packet.data_values = std::move(data_values);

    // Read and convert checksum
    uint32_t be_checksum;
    reader.read(be_checksum);
    packet.checksum = hexi::endian::big_to_native(be_checksum);

    return packet;
}

ProtocolHeader extract_protocol_header(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::allocators::default_allocator alloc;
    hexi::buffers::raw_buffer read_buf(buffer.data(), buffer.size());
    hexi::streams::binary_stream_reader reader(read_buf, alloc);
    ProtocolHeader header;

    // Extract message_id using span and convert
    auto msg_span = reader.span(sizeof(uint32_t));
    header.message_id = hexi::endian::big_to_native(
        *reinterpret_cast<const uint32_t*>(msg_span.data())
    );
    reader.skip(sizeof(uint32_t)); // Skip the header bytes

    return header;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <hexi/endian.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <string>
#include <string_view>
#include <span>

struct MeasurementData {
    uint32_t sensor_id;
    std::vector<float> temperatures;
    double pressure;
    
    void serialise(auto& adaptor) const {
        adaptor.write(sensor_id);
        
        uint32_t size = static_cast<uint32_t>(temperatures.size());
        adaptor.write(size);
        
        for (float temp : temperatures) {
            float le_temp = hexi::endian::conditional_reverse(temp, std::endian::native, std::endian::little);
            adaptor.write(le_temp);
        }
        
        double le_pressure = hexi::endian::conditional_reverse(pressure, std::endian::native, std::endian::little);
        adaptor.write(le_pressure);
    }
    
    void serialise(auto& adaptor) {
        adaptor.read(sensor_id);
        
        uint32_t size;
        adaptor.read(size);
        
        temperatures.resize(size);
        for (uint32_t i = 0; i < size; ++i) {
            float temp;
            adaptor.read(temp);
            hexi::endian::little_to_native_inplace(temp);
            temperatures[i] = temp;
        }
        
        adaptor.read(pressure);
        hexi::endian::little_to_native_inplace(pressure);
    }
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_measurement_data(const MeasurementData& data) {
    hexi::dynamic_buffer buf;
    hexi::buffer_adaptor adaptor(buf);
    data.serialise(adaptor);
    return std::vector<uint8_t>(buf.begin(), buf.end());
}

MeasurementData deserialize_measurement_data(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::dynamic_buffer buf;
    buf.insert(buf.end(), buffer.begin(), buffer.end());
    hexi::buffer_adaptor adaptor(buf);
    MeasurementData data;
    adaptor.serialise(data);
    return data;
}

struct SensorInfo {
    uint32_t sensor_id;
    std::string_view sensor_name;
};

SensorInfo extract_sensor_info(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::dynamic_buffer buf;
    buf.insert(buf.end(), buffer.begin(), buffer.end());
    hexi::buffer_adaptor adaptor(buf);
    
    std::string_view sensor_name = "";
    if (adaptor.view(sensor_name)) {
        // Successfully read null-terminated string
    }
    
    adaptor.enforce_read_bounds(sizeof(uint32_t));
    
    uint32_t sensor_id;
    adaptor.read(sensor_id);
    
    adaptor.skip(adaptor.bytes_remaining());
    
    return {sensor_id, sensor_name};
}
